approach: root cause is upstream in the pinned differ's scenario signature, not in this repo's classifier wrapper; fixed there as one rule (all step arguments), not at the DocString site the issue named
this repo's change is additive-only: a new classification scenario in the frozen spec-gate.feature (self-clears, no re-open) plus a live regression test and the version pin bump
over-fire measured against the whole corpus before proposing the stricter signature: 135 files, 2878 scenarios, zero re-classified
impl gate is blocked on an owner-held npm publish; stop and report rather than land a red or a pin that cannot resolve

why: seven additive scenarios in the frozen spec-gate.feature (classifier reports ADDITIVE 7 added / 0 modified / 0 removed, so the freeze self-clears and no re-open was needed); cold spec-judge ALIGNED on round 4 after three FAIL rounds, each finding the same shape one order out — a prose claim about the scenario identity that no scenario could register a miss on; closed by enumerating the argument-kind by face matrix rather than patching the site each judge named; the round-4 judge derived the matrix independently from the implementation, built nine mutants and measured that every scenario catches at least one while a pristine control survives all seven; mechanical check-suite clean and the pre-existing check-spec-state failures (43 at HEAD, 43 here, none in this node) are untouched

what: the structural differ cannot see a Rule:-nested scenario at all — it reports an empty scenario list, which the classifier reads as no-content-change, so every scenario under a Rule: is outside the freeze; Background: steps are likewise never diffed. A different mechanism from this CR's signature defect: traversal, not identity. Measured latent — no corpus file uses Rule: today, eight use Background:
evidence: reproduced on a clean fixture: a file whose only scenario sits under a Rule: reports an empty scenario list with addOnly true while both its Background step and its nested scenario's Then were rewritten

what: a scenario's tags, its Examples table, its step keywords and its name are all part of the differ's identity but no frozen scenario registers a miss on any of them, so an implementation that dropped one would report unchanged and self-clear with nothing to catch it. Pre-dates this CR. Same freeze-integrity class as this CR: removing a @rubric tag or editing a @trigger Examples row are the live cases
evidence: raised as an independent observation by the cold spec-judge after it derived the identity matrix from the implementation and compared it against the frozen set

what: the frozen scenarios cover a REWRITTEN step argument but not a DELETED one, which is the reported threat model's strongest form — a rubric removed outright rather than gutted. The implementation already handles it and is tested upstream, so this is an unfrozen claim rather than a live hole
evidence: the cold spec-judge measured deletion as correctly reporting modified with addOnly false for both argument kinds, and found no scenario that would register a miss on it

why: cold impl-judge re-derived each of the seven frozen scenarios' oracles independently before reading the tests, and passed all seven with the suite at 27/27 and root verify 21/21; it re-ran the pin ablation itself and reproduced the claim exactly (the three rewrite scenarios fail on the old pin, the four cosmetic controls hold on both), and dumped every fixture pair to confirm each differs only in the axis its scenario names — the tag-delta contamination that slipped past an earlier ablation in this mission is absent; its orthogonal read found the deliberate non-bump of the universal-plugin strings is protected by that project's own frozen suite, so bumping them would have been a narrowing edit on another project's freeze

what: the edit-class classifier's header comment cites the differ's dependency design note by a relative path that resolves to nothing — the path reads as if it sits in the spec tree while the file ships inside the plugin. Pre-dates this CR. Not a typo with one right answer: a shipped skill script referencing a repo-only spec document is a self-containment question, since the spec tree is not part of the published package
evidence: raised by the cold impl-judge's orthogonal read; traced to the commit that introduced the header, not to this CR, so the referenced-artifact check does not gate it
